title: Deep Ordinal Regression Network for Monocular Depth Estimation (DORN)
core_idea: Ordinal regression for depth: encodes depth as increasing binary classification of ordinal labels, avoiding metric regression limitations. Uses dilated ResNet-101 backbone with ASPP.
key_contribution: First to apply ordinal regression to depth estimation; multi-scale feature fusion with side outputs at multiple resolutions.
relevance: This is the baseline model in this project (scripts/dorn.py). AbsRel=0.121 on NYU v2.

title: Towards Robust Monocular Depth Estimation: Mixing Datasets for Zero-shot Cross-dataset Transfer
core_idea: Mixes multiple depth datasets (MegaDepth, DIML, DIODE, etc.) for training. Uses dense prediction transformer (DPT) architecture with Vision Transformer encoder.
key_contribution: Demonstrates that mixing diverse datasets improves zero-shot transfer; MiDaS v3 achieves SOTA with DPT-large.
relevance: Dataset mixing is a potential improvement direction for the agent.

title: Vision Transformers for Dense Prediction (DPT)
core_idea: Uses Vision Transformer (ViT) as encoder with reassembly modules that fuse tokens at different resolutions into full-resolution feature maps.
key_contribution: Replaces CNN encoders with ViT for depth prediction; achieves SOTA with DPT-Hybrid using DINOv1 pretraining.
relevance: ViT-based architecture is a strong alternative to DORN's ResNet backbone.

title: MiDaS: Representing Data with Maximum Depth
core_idea: Estimates relative/inverse depth using scale-and-shift-invariant loss. Uses EfficientNet-Large encoder. Key insight: learning affine-invariant depth avoids metric scale ambiguity.
key_contribution: Scale-and-shift-invariant loss function enables training on heterogeneous mixed datasets.
relevance: The scale-invariant loss idea can be adopted to improve DORN training.

title: Boosting Monocular Depth with Panoramic View (OmniDepth)
core_idea: Uses panoramic context aggregation modules to capture wider spatial context for depth estimation.
key_contribution: Multi-scale panoramic attention for global depth consistency.
relevance: Context aggregation improvements applicable to DORN's decoder.

title: Depth Anything: Unleashing the Power of Large-Scale Unlabeled Data
core_idea: Trains on 62M unlabeled images using a teacher-student self-training framework. DINOv2 encoder with lightweight depth head. Fine-tune on labeled data for metric depth.
key_contribution: Massive unlabeled data pretraining dramatically improves monocular depth; sets new SOTA across multiple benchmarks.
relevance: SOTA baseline (2024). If agent proposes improvements, must beat or approach AbsRel=0.094.

title: ZoeDepth: Combining relative and metric depth
core_idea: Combines relative depth pretraining (MiDaS) with metric bins. Two-stage: relative pretraining then metric bin encoding.
key_contribution: Metric bins bridge the gap between relative and absolute depth estimation.
relevance: Metric bin idea is compatible with DORN's ordinal regression framework.

title: Adabins: Depth Estimation Using Adaptive Bins
core_idea: Adaptively computes depth bins per image using a mini-network. Each image gets its own bin centers rather than fixed bins.
key_contribution: Adaptive bin computation improves over fixed-bin ordinal regression; EfficientNet-b5 encoder.
relevance: Adaptive bins directly improve on DORN's fixed ordinal bins. Strong baseline improvement direction.

title: NeW CRFs: Neural Window Fully-connected CRFs for Monocular Depth Estimation
core_idea: Uses fully-connected CRFs over neural windows to model long-range depth dependencies. Swin Transformer backbone.
key_contribution: CRF-based post-processing with windows captures global structure.
relevance: CRF refinement is a post-processing improvement applicable to any depth model output.

title: PixelFormer: Single Image to Pixel-Symmetric Feature
core_idea: Uses pixel-level attention with symmetric feature aggregation. Swin Transformer-V2 backbone with hierarchical masked transformer decoder.
key_contribution: Symmetric attention mechanism reduces depth artifacts; multi-scale masked decoder.
relevance: Pixel-level attention improvements; 2022 SOTA at AbsRel=0.090.

title: Indoor Depth Estimation with Edge-Aware Loss
core_idea: Edge-aware loss functions (gradient difference, SSIM-based) reduce blurring at object boundaries in indoor depth maps.
key_contribution: Gradient loss + SSIM combination smooths interiors while preserving edges.
relevance: Loss function modification is low-hanging fruit for the agent to try on DORN.

title: GLPDepth: Global-Local Path Network for Depth Estimation
core_idea: Global-Local Path encoder-decoder architecture combining global features from deep layers and local detail from shallow layers. Swin Transformer encoder.
key_contribution: Explicit global-local feature fusion paths; strong baseline architecture.
relevance: Architecture pattern for improving DORN's feature pyramid.
